ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "zip_id" TEXT /* Zip code of the patient's residence */,
  "dob_mm" INTEGER /* Month of birth of the patient */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the patient */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the patient's residence */,
  "education" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed by the patient */,
  "education_code" INTEGER /* Code for the highest level of education completed */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the patient */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the patient */,
  "income" REAL /* Annual income of the patient */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medical_expenses" (
  "expense_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medical expense record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the medical expense record */,
  "income_tax_return" INTEGER /* Income tax return of the patient */,
  "family_size" INTEGER /* Family size of the patient */,
  "total_erop_expenses" REAL /* Total expenses for emergency room outpatient (EROP) visits */,
  "total_obop_expenses" REAL /* Total expenses for office and outpatient (OBOP) visits */,
  "total_op_expenses" REAL /* Total expenses for all outpatient visits */,
  "expense_year" INTEGER /* Year the medical expense was incurred */,
  "expense_category" TEXT /* Category of the medical expense (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expense_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_expenses_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "insurance_coverage" (
  "coverage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each insurance coverage record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the insurance coverage record */,
  "insurance_coverage" TEXT /* Type of insurance coverage the patient has */,
  "has_usc" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the patient has USC coverage */,
  "coverage_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the insurance coverage */,
  "coverage_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the insurance coverage */,
  "premium_amount" REAL /* Monthly premium amount for the insurance coverage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("coverage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_insurance_coverage_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare service record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient associated with the healthcare service record */,
  "total_erop_visits" INTEGER /* Total number of emergency room outpatient (EROP) visits */,
  "total_obop_visits" INTEGER /* Total number of office and outpatient (OBOP) visits */,
  "total_op_visits" INTEGER /* Total number of all outpatient visits */,
  "ip_visits" INTEGER /* Number of inpatient (IP) visits */,
  "ip_expenses" REAL /* Total expenses for inpatient (IP) visits */,
  "service_date" TEXT /* Date the healthcare service was utilized */,
  "service_category" TEXT /* Category of the healthcare service (e.g., primary care, specialist, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_services_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "data_source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the data source is updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "dataset_description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source associated with the dataset */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data in the dataset (e.g., medical expenses, insurance coverage, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset associated with the version */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the permission */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset associated with the permission */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission granted to the user (e.g., read, write, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the research team */,
  "team_description" TEXT /* Description of the research team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team member */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the research team the member belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is a member of the team */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the team member (e.g., lead researcher, data analyst, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("member_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_members_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_members_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES research_teams ("team_id")
)
